        buildEnvPrefix
      • buildEnvPrefix(forwardAll: bool; vars: seq[string]): string

Source   Edit  

Common types, constants, and global variables for npsh.

Imports

config

Vars

dryRun: bool = false
Whether to perform a dry run (just show what would be done) Source   Edit   
envAll: bool = false
Whether to forward all env vars to remote hosts Source   Edit   
noEnv: bool = false
Whether to disable env var forwarding entirely Source   Edit   
prefixOutput: bool = false
Whether to prefix output with host names Source   Edit   
runOnAllHosts: bool = false
Whether to run command on all configured hosts Source   Edit   
testMode: bool = false
Whether to run in test mode (execute 'true' command) Source   Edit   
useStdin: bool = false
Whether to read from stdin and pipe to remote command Source   Edit   
workDir: string = ""
Working directory for remote command execution Source   Edit  

Consts

DefaultConfigPath = "/home/runner/.npsh"
Source   Edit   
EnvBlacklist = ["SSH_CLIENT", "SSH_CONNECTION", "SSH_TTY", "SSH_AUTH_SOCK",
                "SSH_AGENT_PID", "DISPLAY", "WINDOWID", "TERM_SESSION_ID",
                "XDG_RUNTIME_DIR", "XDG_SESSION_ID", "XDG_SEAT", "XDG_VTNR",
                "DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS", "PATH", "LD_LIBRARY_PATH",
                "NIX_PATH"]
Source   Edit   
TabWidth = 8
Source   Edit  

Procs

proc buildEnvPrefix(forwardAll: bool; vars: seq[string]): string {....raises: [],
    tags: [ReadEnvEffect], forbids: [].}
Build env var prefix string for remote command. Source   Edit   
proc formatHostPrefix(hostname: string; maxHostLen: int): string {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Format hostname prefix with proper alignment. Source   Edit   
proc parseHosts(hostsStr: string): seq[string] {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Parse comma-separated host list. Source   Edit   
proc resolveHosts(hostSpecs: seq[string]; allHosts: seq[Host]): seq[string] {.
    ...raises: [ValueError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Resolve host specifications (hostnames or node IDs) to hostnames. Source   Edit
